content.introduction: Bucket sort distributes elements into value-range buckets, sorts each bucket, and concatenates results.

sections.title: 7.9.1 Key Ideas and Model
sections.content: Define bucket mapping function and assumptions about input distribution for expected efficiency.
examples: Map floating-point values in [0,1) to bucket indices
Choose bucket count relative to input size

sections.title: 7.9.2 Representation and Workflow
sections.content: Describe bucket allocation, insertion, intra-bucket sorting, and merge-back process.
examples: Sort each bucket with insertion sort
Concatenate buckets in order to produce final output

sections.title: 7.9.3 Complexity and Practical Guidance
sections.content: Expected time can approach O(n) with near-uniform distribution; worst case degrades when data clusters heavily.
examples: Use bucket sort for normalized numeric datasets

keyTakeaways: Design bucket mapping and count strategy
Apply bucket sort where value ranges are known
